title: leontodon saxatilis subsp. crassifolius
intro: Leontodon saxatilis subsp. crassifolius, also known as the Rough Hawkbit, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the dandelion family that is native to Europe and Western Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it beneficial for treating wounds, skin conditions, and gastrointestinal issues. The health benefits of Leontodon saxatilis subsp. crassifolius include reducing inflammation, promoting digestion, and supporting immune function. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used to treat various health conditions, although it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using any herbal remedy.
